evidence_snippet: Raymund and his rebels fled beyond the Wall to join the free folk.

reason: cannibalism is the reported method of death of his rebels; not a graph-traversable relationship edge from Raymund to the concept
Raymund's head was delivered to Eastwatch posthumously; no relationship edge from the living person to the location
his rebellion included former Faith Militant members but Raymund himself was Kingsguard, not Faith Militant; organization link is to his followers' background, not to Raymund directly
fled toward free folk with intent to join them but was subsequently killed/eaten by wildlings; no sustained alliance or membership relationship
giants killed Walton Stark, not Raymund; not a relationship between Raymund and giants
haunted forest is where Lord Stark was slain pursuing Raymund; not Raymund's location or a direct relationship
Iron Throne mentioned as political background context for Jaehaerys's claim; no direct relationship edge from Raymund to the throne
Jaehaerys offered Raymund the Watch-or-execution choice; indirect disposition arrangement, not a direct service or allegiance edge from Raymund
lords used generically as a title aspiration; no specific claim to a particular Lord title
Lord Commander assigned Raymund's command, but service relationship with the Watch is already captured by SWORN_TO Night's Watch on the infobox

evidence_snippet: Ser Raymund and his sworn brother of the Kingsguard, Ser Olyver Bracken, abandoned King Maegor I Targaryen

reason: Rimegate was Olyver Bracken's command, not Raymund's; mentioned as parallel to Raymund's Sable Hall
